titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Pundits, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 3, 2020 at 09:59

did.abstract: The records consist of correspondence, photographs, pledge sheets, clippings, and miscellaneous files documenting the endeavors of the Yale Pundits.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 40 (YRG 40), Records of Yale clubs, societies, and organizations.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The records were transferred from the Pundits, 1996-2010 and 2015-2016.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is restricted. Written permission of president of organization required.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Pundits, Yale University, Records (RU 308). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Pundits was founded in 1884 by members of the Class of 1887 and survived for one year. It appeared again in 1899, 1903, and in 1994. The plan of its meetings called for the chosen writer "to set up an ideal as an idol," which the other members were to demolish if possible. One of their first deeds was the establishment of a prize for an essay "on some need of the College and how it may be supplied, or on some tendency of a feature contrary to the welfare of the College, and how it may best be amended." Like its predecessors, the Pundits plan and execute pranks directed toward other Yale clubs, societies, and organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of correspondence, photographs, pledge sheets, clippings, and miscellaneous files documenting the endeavors of the Yale Pundits.

did.unittitle: Yale Class Book/Banner info on Pundits

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

did.unittitle: Internal documents re: recruitment and tap
Gift to TBIYTB from 2008 alum [dong bong]
John Gaddis, "Art of Biography Seminar," Titanic prank poster
Mock up copy of Yale Literary Magazine (XXII: I)
Parent's weekend Apple product coupon prank
"Angels" playbill: playwright Otis Blum dedicates show to the Pundits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Undated posters hung on main campus by members of Pundits during a visit by Mr. Charles B. Johnson '54.
